data.title: Golden Web3.0 Daily Report | US SEC concludes years-long investigation into Zcash Foundation
data.description:
DeFi data

1. Total market value of DeFi tokens: $116.497 billion

DeFi Total Market Cap Data Source: coingecko

2. Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours: $59.71

Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours. Data source: coingecko

3. Assets locked in DeFi: $130.371 billionbillion

Top 10 DeFi Projects by Locked Assets and Total Value Locked. Data Source: defillama

NFT Data

1. Total Market Value of NFTs: $117.587 billion

2.24 hours NFT transaction volume 1.932 billion USD

Top NFTs in 24 Hours

Top 10 NFTs by Sales Growth in 24 Hours Data Source: NFTGO

Headlines

US SEC Ends Year-Long Investigation of Zcash Foundation, clarifies No Enforcement Action

Zcash

... The foundation stated that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has concluded its multi-year investigation. The investigation, which began in August 2023, concerned the review of "certain crypto asset offerings," and the SEC has made it clear that it will not recommend any enforcement action. This move comes at a time when the SEC is adjusting its crypto regulatory direction during the Trump administration, having dropped several cases, including Coinbase, and terminated investigations into numerous DeFi projects over the past year.

      MEME Hot Topics

      1. Binance Alpha Launches "Snowball" and "Life K-Line"

      Golden Finance reports that Binance Alpha has launched the Chinese Meme coins "Snowball" and "Life K-Line".

        2. CZ: The most important ability for on-chain meme players is "risk control"

        According to Jinse Finance, Binance founder CZ responded to community questions by stating that for an on-chain meme player who lacks information advantage, influence, and is not a project team, the most important ability is "risk control".

        DeFi Hot Topics

        1. MilkyWay Protocol Announces Gradual Cessation of Operations and Permanent Shutdown

        On January 15th, MilkyWay Protocol announced that it is gradually ceasing operations and will permanently shut down.

        MilkyWay stated that the demand and adoption of decentralized finance (DeFi) did not meet expectations, and the WayCard launch was too late, failing to alleviate funding pressure in time. MilkyWay's revenue mainly comes from liquidity staking fees, of which the protocol retains a 10% share. As part of the shutdown process, these earned protocol fees will be returned to MILK token holders by distributing USDC proportionally to eligible snapshot holders. According to Jinse Finance, the BNB Chain Foundation will continue to expand project support and will conduct more asset acquisitions in the future. Currently, BNB Chain is soliciting suggestions for further asset acquisitions.

          3. Crypto Derivatives Exchange Bitnomial Launches First Regulated Aptos Futures in the US

          According to Jinse Finance, Coin Bureau disclosed that crypto derivatives exchange Bitnomial has launched the first CFTC-regulated Aptos futures contract in the United States and plans to expand to APT perpetual futures and options products soon.

            4. Ethereum PoS Network Staking Exceeds 36 Million ETH, Setting a New All-Time High

            According to Jinse Finance, Coin Bureau disclosed that Ethereum staking has reached a new all-time high. Currently, more than 36 million Ethereum are locked in the Ethereum Proof-of-Stake (PoS) network, accounting for nearly 30% of the circulating supply, with a value of over $118 billion at current prices.

            5. He Yi Responds to Solana and Starknet Activity Controversy: We're All Friends, Let's Keep Harmony

            January 15th news, in response to Solana's mockery of Starknet's "only 8 daily active users and 10 daily transactions," and Starknet's subsequent response, "Who told this little brother Solana the data?", He Yi responded on social media, saying: "Take a deep breath and relax, we're all friends, let's keep harmony." He Yi also added "peanuts, sunflower seeds, and mineral water" to the post, urging the outside world to view the discussion with a more relaxed attitude and avoid excessive controversy caused by comparing on-chain data.

            Disclaimer: Jinse Finance, as a blockchain information platform, publishes articles for informational purposes only and does not constitute actual investment advice. Please establish correct investment concepts and be sure to improve your risk awareness.

data.title: Why has Crypto Twitter (CT) become a ghost town?
data.description:
Author: Bruce Source: X, @BruceBlue

Do you feel this sense of disconnect?

BTC is above $90,000, ETF funds are still pouring in, and @MicroStrategy is still buying. But when you open Twitter, you find it deserted, like a ghost town. KOLs who used to get tons of retweets for a simple tick now get very few likes; Alpha signals that used to guarantee price increases are now "sell signals."

Many people attribute this to @elonmusk's algorithm adjustments or Shadowban (rate limiting).

Completely wrong!!!

Look at this chart.

This is a chart showing the trend of new followers of major crypto KOLs overlaid with the BTC price (white line). This chart not only reveals where the traffic went, but also explains why the altseason you've been waiting for is so late. Asset prices hit new highs, but attention is dropping to zero. The most striking contrast in this chart is: In 2021: as the white line (price) rose, the colored stacked area (new users/new followers) exploded simultaneously. Back then, the price increase itself was the best advertisement; every new high brought thousands of new investors eager to get rich quick. They registered accounts, frantically followed KOLs and influencers, and searched for the next 100x coin. 2024-2025: The white line (price) has broken through to the sky, but the colored area at the bottom is almost a straight line. The conclusion is brutal: This is a bull market with almost no retail investors (The Retail-Less Bull). Prices are hitting new highs because BlackRock and institutions are buying, and because sovereign wealth funds are allocating. But no new retail investors are rushing in. Why aren't people scrolling through Twitter anymore? Don't blame the algorithm anymore. The fundamental reason for the disappearance of traffic is the collapse of trust and the migration of user behavior. The End of Survivorship Bias: In the massive surge of cryptocurrency trading in 2021, the vast majority perished in the subsequent collapses of LUNA, FTX, and Celsius. This wasn't a temporary respite; it was a permanent departure. They uninstalled exchanges, unfollowed KOLs, and even the word "crypto" triggered PTSD. The Depletion of Alpha: In 2021, Twitter was the source of information. Following a prominent figure could genuinely generate Alpha. But now, Twitter has become a source of noise. When you see a ticker flooding Twitter, it often means you might be the one left holding the bag. Tools Replace Social Media: The remaining hardcore players no longer need to listen to KOLs' ghost stories. They directly monitor on-chain data (GMGN, DexScreener). Since everyone's just profiting at each other's expense, looking at the data is more useful than looking at Twitter. The real logic behind the disappearance of the altcoin season: the PvP dilemma. Many veteran investors are still clinging to outdated ideas: waiting for BTC to finish rising, for funds to overflow, and then rotating to ETH, and then to altcoins. This chart tells you: stop dreaming. The logic in 2021: a continuous influx of new money (new followers). The big influencers made money, the new followers took over, liquidity overflowed layer by layer, and all coins soared. The current logic: since there are no new followers (the colored area disappears), the current market is a zero-sum game. These thousand people are just taking from each other's pockets (PvP). Institutions bought Bitcoin, but they won't buy your meme or those outdated VC coins. And since retail investors aren't entering the market, who will buy your altcoin? This is why altcoin liquidity is so scarce right now: there are no buyers, only retail investors fighting each other. Only by recognizing reality can we survive. This chart gives us two extremely important insights: Embrace core assets: Incremental funds come from institutions, and institutions only buy core assets (BTC/BNB, etc.). Follow the pace of incremental funds, instead of fantasizing about a retail investor frenzy. Abandon the illusion of traffic: If you're still trading based on Twitter recommendations, you're using a 2021 map to navigate a 2026 maze. Crypto Twitter didn't die from its algorithm; it died from the most fundamental characteristic of this bull market: it's a game for Wall Street and professional institutions, while retail investors are left behind in the previous cycle.

data.title: Why is the Chinese Meme Coin so popular?
data.description:
Author: 0xGrandpa

I. What We Are Witnessing

The crypto market in early 2026 is experiencing an unprecedented cultural reversal:

Western traders are searching for the exact meaning of "我踏马来了" (I'm coming!) on platform X, trying to understand why these five Chinese characters can support a token with a market capitalization of hundreds of millions of dollars. Memes like "老子" (Laozi) and "斌安人生" (Binance Life) have become daily topics in the global crypto community.

This is the first time that Chinese subculture has gone global on a large scale in the form of financial assets, and it's also the first time we've seen an emoji or a slang term absorb hundreds of millions of dollars in real funds within 72 hours.

However, these tokens have no traditional value support. There are no white papers, no technical teams, no application scenarios, and not even a complete narrative.

But their market capitalization can surge from tens of thousands of dollars to hundreds of millions of dollars in 72 hours, or plummet by half in three hours. All traditional financial valuation tools—price-to-earnings ratio, cash flow, technological roadmap—become ineffective here. Instead, a completely new pricing logic emerges: attention equals value, consensus equals truth, and emotion equals liquidity. He Yi replied on X with "Wishing you a Binance life," and four days later, the token's market capitalization reached $524 million. On-chain data shows that some people entered at $3,000, and their book value rose to $1.6 million. Countless others chased the highs, only to lose everything in the crash. Of course, this is not a simple speculative bubble. The word "bubble" is too mild; it implies a kind of "irrational" deviation, as if simply regaining one's rationality would bring everything back to normal. But the problem is: when consensus itself is the only anchor, when everyone is playing the same zero-sum game, what is "normal"? The K-line chart of Meme Coin is the most honest emotional recorder of this era. It records not only price fluctuations, but also the anxiety, fervor, and disillusionment of this generation—some found an opportunity to change their fate here, while many more were left with a permanent record of losses. II. From Internet Joke to Financial Asset In December 2013, IBM engineer Billy Markus and Adobe employee Jackson Palmer spent two hours copying and pasting the Bitcoin code, changing the logo to a Shiba Inu, and naming it Dogecoin. Their initial intention was to satirize the then-prevalent cryptocurrency speculation frenzy, but this "joke" resulted in a 300% increase within 72 hours of its launch, with numerous tipping activities appearing on Reddit, where people used it to buy pizzas for strangers and tip content creators. Dogecoin's success proves a disruptive fact: in decentralized finance systems, consensus itself can be a source of value. You don't even need to create actual products or solve specific problems; as long as enough people believe it has value, it truly has value. And the most effective tools for building consensus are precisely those cultural symbols that can evoke collective emotions. From 2013 to 2021, Meme Coin remained on the fringes for a long time. Early imitators like Nyancoin (Rainbow Cat) and Coinye (a tribute to Kanye West) mostly faded quickly, failing due to over-reliance on the power of a single symbol and a lack of community building and narrative evolution. The turning point came in 2021 when SHIB (Shiba Inu Coin) saw a more than 100,000-fold increase in value within six months. More importantly, SHIB proved that latecomers could seize market share through "narrative layering"—grafting new storylines onto existing memes. In 2023, the emergence of one-click token issuance platforms like Pump.fun ushered in the industrialized production stage for meme coins. Thousands of new coins were born daily on the Solana chain, from PEPE's "Sad Frog" to various AI concepts and zoo-themed coins, exhibiting unprecedented fragmentation and short-cycle characteristics in the market. However, at this stage, Meme coins were essentially still a derivative of Western internet culture, with the Chinese community being more followers than creators. The 2026 Chinese Meme coin craze represented a new mutation. It no longer merely imitated Western Doge or Pepe, but directly transformed native Chinese internet memes—the crude expression "我踏马来了" (I'm here!), the auspicious phrase "斌安人生" (Binance Life), and the playful adaptation of traditional cultural symbols like "老子" (Laozi)—into tradable financial assets. Behind this transformation was a collective counterattack from the Chinese crypto community after years of "cultural discounting": We no longer needed to explain to the West why the meme was funny, because consensus operated within our own language system. III. The Real Force Driving This Frenzy Psychological Mechanism: Dopamine and the Fantasy of Changing Fate Among all the factors driving the Meme coin craze, the psychological mechanism occupies the most central position. This is not "irrationality" in the traditional sense, but a rational choice amplified in a specific environment—when the path to wealth accumulation in the real world becomes increasingly rigid, and when the rate of return on traditional investments continues to decline, the "extreme odds" offered by Meme coin become an irresistible psychological temptation. Looking at the data on "Binance Life" makes this clear. On October 4, 2025, this token was listed on BNB Chain. He Yi replied to the community with the phrase "Wishing you a Binance Life," and CZ followed suit. In just four days, the token's market capitalization surged from an extremely low level to $524 million, with early participants gaining over 6000 times their initial investment. On-chain data shows that address 0x8844 invested only 5 BNB (approximately $3,000), and within days, its account value rose to $1.6 million. This "$3,000 to $1.6 million" wealth myth has an impact far exceeding any rational argument; it directly breaks down the psychological defenses of ordinary investors. When social media is filled with screenshots of "someone turning $85 into $140,000," and when stories of "smart money making a fortune" are repeatedly circulated, people systematically overestimate their chances of becoming the next lucky one, ignoring those silent losers. This cognitive bias is amplified to the extreme in the Meme coin market. The transparency of blockchain makes the "wealth creation myth" verifiable, while stories of losses are often drowned out by the noise. A deeper driving force stems from emotional compensation. Against the backdrop of slowing global economic growth and solidified social mobility, people face the dual pressures of present anxieties and future uncertainties. Buying Meme coins, to some extent, carries the symbolic meaning of a "ticket to change one's destiny." While traditional investment requires long-term accumulation, risk hedging, and professional knowledge, Meme coins only require "clicking the mouse at the right time." This simplified decision-making path itself provides psychological comfort—it makes people believe that wealth can be acquired without relying on existing resources and social networks. But the most brutal aspect is the game structure itself. The Meme coin market is almost a purely PvP (player versus player) environment, where every profit corresponds to a loss for the other party. This zero-sum game fosters a unique "chain of suspicion": I worry you'll sell first, you worry I'll sell first, and everyone is guessing "when will others leave?" This ultimately leads to a paradoxical result—the more rational the trader, the more likely they are to flee during the first surge. This is because they understand that market consensus is fragile and liquidity depletion is instantaneous. This is why most Meme coins exhibit a "peak at opening" trend: all participants are playing the same "musical chairs" game, but there aren't enough chairs from the start. Propagation Mechanism: Attention Hegemony and Traffic Pricing If psychological mechanisms explain "why people are willing to buy," the propagation mechanism answers "why this coin and not that coin." In a market environment where thousands of new tokens are born every day, propagation efficiency becomes a crucial competitive barrier. The advantages of Chinese Meme Coin's dissemination lie primarily in the contagiousness of its symbols. The five characters "我踏马来了" (I'm coming!) encapsulate the direct expression and emotional catharsis unique to the Chinese internet, conveying a "breaking the mold" attitude without requiring complex cultural background. The traditional cultural symbol "Laozi" (老子) is imbued with a multifaceted meaning in the modern context, encompassing both self-deprecation and arrogance. "Binance Life" (斌安人生) cleverly transforms a commercial brand into a blessing, forming an intimate and playful community code. These symbols share common characteristics: visual simplicity (suitable for emojis and logos), emotional richness (quickly evoking resonance), and semantic ambiguity (leaving room for secondary creation). However, symbols are only the starting point; what truly determines the depth of dissemination is the traffic pricing mechanism. In the world of Meme Coin, there's a cruel but true formula: Traffic = Valuation. A reply from He Yi on the X platform, an emoji from CZ—these seemingly casual interactions actually inject "liquidity expectations" into a certain token. The "Binance Life" case is the best proof: after He Yi's tweet on October 4th, the token saw an influx of over 14 whale addresses holding more than a million US dollars within 24 hours, and the number of on-chain transactions surged by 300%. The endorsement of a top KOL creates a kind of consensus—everyone assumes that others will buy because of this tweet, so they rush to buy, ultimately forming a self-fulfilling prophecy. Even more alarming is that this kind of dissemination has formed an efficient cross-platform closed loop. A message first spreads within WeChat or Telegram groups, then appears in "educational" posts on Xiaohongshu (a Chinese social media platform), followed by discussion on the English community of platform X (often accompanied by machine translation misinterpretations and derivative works). On-chain data shows whales entering the market, and then screenshots of exchange candlestick charts are shared on social media. A new wave of FOMO (fear of missing out) is ignited. The entire chain can be completed in 6-12 hours, so fast that any "calm analysis" seems sluggish and powerless. However, there is a communication paradox here: When the spread of Meme coin reaches its peak—for example, by trending on Weibo or being reported by mainstream financial media—it often means that the attention dividend has been exhausted. Because the most valuable stage in the Meme coin's lifecycle is the period when "small-scale consensus is spreading," not the moment when "everyone knows about it." Once the spread crosses the critical point, the market enters a state of "depletion of buyers," because everyone who could potentially be persuaded has already entered the market. This is why experienced traders choose to exit when mainstream media begins reporting on it; at this point, the understanding spread curve has peaked. Social Mentality: The "Anti-VC" Narrative and Virtual Tribes The rise of Meme coin can be understood from a sociological perspective as a symbolic resistance of "grassroots finance" against "elite capital." This resistance is not an organized movement, but a collective expression spontaneously formed through market behavior. The "anti-VC narrative" is the most explicit manifestation of this resistance. In traditional crypto projects, venture capital (VC) firms typically acquire large amounts of tokens at extremely low prices in the early stages, then slowly cash out on the open market through mechanisms like lock-up and release, leaving retail investors at the bottom of the value chain from the outset. Meme Coin's launch model (everyone buying at almost the same time and price) is imbued with a certain moral superiority—it symbolizes a rejection of the traditional financial hierarchy. "Binance Life," as the pioneering project of this wave of Chinese Meme coin launches, owes its popularity partly to its origins on the grassroots platform Four.Meme, rather than through private placements or pre-sales. This is a crucial component of community consensus. A deeper social psychology lies in the identity recognition of "virtual tribalization." When someone buys "Laozi," they gain not only holdings but also joining a virtual tribe sharing specific cultural symbols. Within this tribe, people reinforce their sense of belonging by proclaiming "I am the cultural ceiling of Chinese Memes," and demonstrate loyalty by creating emojis and participating in community voting. This tribal identity is the sociological basis for Meme Coin's survival during market crashes—even if the price halves, as long as community consensus remains, the token retains the narrative space for a "comeback." However, we must be aware that this narrative itself is full of contradictions. In practice, it is often manipulated by a small number of "smart money" individuals who possess informational and financial advantages. Ordinary investors who enter the market after seeing news on social media may have already entered at prices tens or even hundreds of times higher than those who bought in early. The real rules of the game remain in the hands of those at the top of the information asymmetry pyramid. The Philosophical Underlying Factor: The Financialization of Nihilism A core issue of the Meme coin phenomenon is: when we pay real money for a digital symbol with no practical value, what are we actually buying? Traditional financial theory states that asset value derives from the discounted value of future cash flows, or is based on scarcity and use value. But Meme coin completely overturns this logic—it neither generates cash flow nor possesses practical functions (it doesn't even possess the most basic attribute of cryptocurrency, such as a "payment tool"). Its entire value lies in the circular argument that "others also believe it has value." This value structure echoes Jean Baudrillard's theory of "simulacra": in postmodern society, symbols have detached themselves from the real objects they refer to, becoming self-referential "simulacra." Meme Coin is a pure simulacrum: it doesn't represent any value creation in the real economy, but when enough people believe it has value, it does possess real exchange value at this very moment. This logic of "consensus is truth" is both absurd and irrefutable—because in a decentralized, unregulated market, the right to define value itself is decentralized. From a more radical perspective, the popularity of Meme Coin reflects a kind of "financialization of nihilism." When grand narratives collapse, when people no longer believe that technological progress will inevitably lead to a better future, and when "long-term value investing" is repeatedly proven to be unable to outrun luck and timing, "meaninglessness" itself becomes a kind of meaning. This mentality is particularly prevalent among Generation Z investors, who grew up in the low-growth era following the financial crisis and naturally lack trust in the narrative of "steady wealth accumulation," willing to accept high-risk, high-volatility games. Fourth, the essence of finance: Who really makes money in this game? Returning to the most practical question: In this frenzy, who is making money? Who is losing money? First, the traditional financial valuation framework seems to fail here. Tools like P/E (price-to-earnings ratio) and P/B (price-to-book ratio) cannot explain why a token with no cash flow can be worth hundreds of millions of dollars. Social media mentions, KOL influence, and community activity become the benchmarks. When a token's market capitalization far exceeds what its attention can support—it often foreshadows a sharp short-term correction. Second, we must be wary of "liquidity traps." When the Meme coin narrative reaches its emotional peak—with widespread online discussion and mainstream media coverage—it's a peak moment in the sense of communication studies. However, this is precisely the turning point where financial liquidity begins to dry up, because all potential buyers have entered the market, leaving only existing funds engaged in a game of cat and mouse. Any slight disturbance can trigger a stampede of selling. The "He Yi account theft incident" on December 10, 2025, is a typical example. Hackers used the stolen account to publish false recommendations, causing a short-term surge in a certain token's price. The hacker invested approximately 19,000 USDT through two new wallets, cashing out about $43,000 at the peak, making a profit of about $55,000. But when CZ (Cai Xing) refuted the claims on X (a cryptocurrency exchange), the coin plummeted 78% within 30 minutes, trapping countless buyers who had chased the high. In the Meme coin market, consensus takes time to build, but collapse can happen in an instant. Finally, we must see the true nature of this game. The financial essence of Meme Coin remains a high-risk, zero-sum game. Behind those stories of "$85 turning into $140,000" are hundreds of silent victims whose assets have been wiped out. On-chain data records everything: behind the 14 million-dollar profit addresses on "Binance Life" are over 47,000 loss addresses, with a median loss of 62%. In a system where there is no value creation, only value transfer, the concentration of wealth is inevitable. V. What Crossroads Are We Standing At? The Chinese Meme Coin craze of 2026 is essentially a sensor of the emotions of the times. It records the anxiety, humor, rebellion, and greed of this generation in the most straightforward and brutal way. In an era where certainty is increasingly scarce, people crave extreme uncertainty to offset the mediocrity of life; in a society where social mobility is rigid, even a gamble where most are destined to lose is more attractive than accepting fate. This craze will eventually subside, like all speculative bubbles in history. But the traces it leaves will not disappear. Consensus can be financialized, cultural symbols can be priced, and attention can be traded. Regardless of how we evaluate all of this, one thing is certain—we have entered an unprecedented era in which the definition of value is being rewritten.

data.title: Former New York City Mayor Embroiled in Rug Pull Scandal
data.description:
Author: Beosin

On January 13-14, former New York City Mayor Eric Adams faced significant scrutiny due to the sharp price drop of his token NYC after its launch. The Beosin security team conducted a detailed on-chain analysis of this incident and shares the results below:

Event Summary

On January 13, Beijing time, former New York City Mayor Eric Adams announced the launch of the NYC token on social media (token contract address: Ho5fkxk9uqUuVN5kt7TUFacduWCpKPAuYkM9HD3KAXA2).

... However, 30 minutes after the token's listing, the liquidity pool's creation address, 9Ty4M4t9MZArhJBKFWbYrzeqdMf2Ua5eHWSRW3LXKyGt, began removing liquidity: This address removed approximately 2,377,376 USDC, causing the NYC token price to drop by more than [amount missing]. 80%, with its market capitalization shrinking from a peak of $600 million to less than $100 million. On January 15th, Adams continued to promote the NYC token by retweeting tweets, and through a statement from spokesperson Todd Shapiro, stated that he himself had not profited from or removed funds from the NYC token, but the community remained skeptical. On-chain Data Analysis Using BeosinTrace to analyze the Solana address 9Ty4M4t9MZArhJBKFWbYrzeqdMf2Ua5eHWSRW3LXKyGt, we can obtain relevant results regarding its on-chain behavior. 1. On-chain Activity Overview Address 9Ty4M obtained 1,000,001 USDC from the Mayan cross-chain bridge and a Kraken user address, and subsequently created an NYC-USDC liquidity pool. After the price plummeted due to the removal of liquidity at 6:43 AM Beijing time on January 13th, address 9Ty4M has been continuously buying small amounts of NYC tokens since 4:15 PM that day, and continues to do so until now. Further tracing of the aforementioned cross-chain funds reveals that they originated from the Arbitrum address 0xBc985b167C5F3801B19E2Dec7082c2fcbBF07e91. Further investigation shows that 90% of these funds were withdrawn from Kraken, and 10% came from an unknown multi-chain active transaction address 0x276fbb81c8a7567ad2412469a549bd4dc82cab65.

2. Transaction Data Analysis

According to data statistics from BeosinTrace, address 9Ty4M has purchased 2,301,250 and 1,566,926 NYC tokens respectively on Jupiter through addresses FWuiWEdkcR1NLPjozcEuErtGGDZG5kyPu3oszmnF8uni and H7Lnoh82SZghsXmoqffoCiFz7U3D1cwZ8ZPjRUMU7fc5, but NYC The price only returned to 0.133-0.134, with a total market capitalization of approximately $130 million. Address 9Ty4M currently holds 1,332,243.85 USDC. Due to the limited liquidity of the NYC token, this address has profited approximately $330,000 without considering NYC token yields. Conclusion This celebrity token issuance event has once again sparked huge controversy. Issuing tokens inherently faces risks related to compliance, technology, and fund security. The removal of liquidity during the event, leading to a sharp drop in token price, has negatively impacted investors and the market. The on-chain data analysis using BeosinTrace above also confirms the existence of "liquidity manipulation." After withdrawing $2.37 million in liquidity, the issuer continued to buy back in, but only returned approximately $1 million, leaving $1.33 million still held in the issuer's address. Users should raise their security awareness and avoid trading such high-risk, easily manipulated tokens. Beosin will continue to monitor token liquidity and the fund dynamics of related addresses to ensure the safety of users' funds.

data.title: With retail investor traffic and institutional investors entering the market, what's next for BNB Chain?
data.description:
Author: Viee I Biteye Content Team

Recently, news of trading function integration on Twitter has sparked a new round of discussions about entry points. Amidst increasingly fierce competition for traffic entry points, BNB Chain has quietly forged a different path and will continue its efforts in 2026.

Looking back over the past few years, BNB Chain has consistently been a popular trading platform for retail investors. Low fees, fast speeds, and a smooth trading experience, coupled with the inherent user traffic from Binance, have made it the starting point for countless users entering the crypto market. In 2025, the Meme coin on BSC experienced another surge in popularity. After the initial hype, BNB Chain did not enter a period of calm; instead, it attracted more traditional institutions to enter the market. BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, CMB International, Circle, and others have successively deployed assets to BNB Chain, forming a new pattern where institutions, retail investors, and projects coexist within the same ecosystem.

... Next, we will reconstruct this process from multiple dimensions, deconstructing how BNB Chain has become an important platform for the symbiosis of TradFi and crypto.

I. In 2025, BNB Chain was no longer just a paradise for crypto retail investors

If we only look at the data, BNB Chain delivered a comprehensive growth report card in 2025:

  • The total number of unique addresses exceeded 700 million, and the number of daily active users exceeded 4 million

  • The average daily transaction volume peaked at 31 million transactions in October

  • These figures demonstrate that BNB Chain has withstood the long-term test of real-world use cases in multiple areas, including stablecoin trading, asset transfer, and high-frequency interaction.

    More perceptible to users than the data itself is the active atmosphere on the chain. In the first half of 2025, driven by the @Four_FORM_ platform, multiple rounds of Meme market trends emerged, with Meme coins such as $FLOKI, $Cheems, and $BROCCOLI experiencing rapid surges, making BNB Chain a hot chain for retail investors once again.

    Starting in the second half of the year, a new wave of activity, represented by Chinese meme coins like $BinanceLife, ignited the enthusiasm of the Chinese community. After the initial surge in popularity, while on-chain meme trading volume declined somewhat, stablecoin popularity continued to rise. Stablecoins such as $USDC, $USDT, $USD1, and $U were widely used in payment, lending, and yield-generating products. Meanwhile, institutions like Circle and BlackRock put stablecoins or money market funds on-chain, with BNB Chain becoming one of the first platforms to do so, demonstrating that assets are gradually being deposited on-chain and retained long-term. In 2025, BNB Chain experienced simultaneous growth in two directions: a cyclical surge in meme traffic and the steady on-chaining of traditional financial assets, along with the continued expansion of the use of stablecoins and real-world yield-generating products. This also signifies that the BNB Chain ecosystem is beginning to support long-term capital application scenarios.

    II. Why BNB Chain: Three Reasons Traditional Institutions Choose It

    In 2025, several heavyweight financial institutions successively launched on BNB Chain, covering almost all sectors including stablecoins, money market funds, and interest-bearing assets:

    • BlackRock: BlackRock, the world's largest asset management company, put its USD cash management fund BUIDL on the blockchain, completed tokenization through Securitize, and listed it on BNB Chain.

    • CMBI: Issued a $3.8 billion money market fund token, CMBMINT, allowing qualified investors to subscribe and redeem on the blockchain.

    • China Merchants Bank International (CMBI): Issued CMBMINT, a money market fund token with a scale of $3.8 billion, allowing qualified investors to subscribe and redeem on the blockchain.

    • Circle: Deploying its interest-bearing stablecoin USYC on BNB Chain. According to RWA.xyz data, the total on-chain supply of Circle's interest-bearing stablecoin USYC has exceeded $1.5 billion, with over $1.4 billion deployed on BNB Chain. [Image: BNB Chain USYC surpasses $1.4 billion] Why are traditional institutions so fond of BNB Chain? Based on these cases, three main reasons can be summarized: 1. Low cost and high performance: BNB Chain is renowned for its superior performance and low gas fees, making it ideal for the needs of high-frequency financial applications. Compared to the Ethereum mainnet, which is prone to high fees and congestion, BNB Chain provides a more user-friendly trading environment, allowing institutional products to operate more efficiently on-chain. 2. Large user base: Leveraging Binance's years of experience, BNB Chain boasts the most diverse user base globally, spanning Asia and emerging markets. This allows institutional assets to reach a wider audience upon deployment. For example, BUIDL can be used as collateral in Binance's system, opening up a market of tens of millions of CEX users. 3. Well-developed infrastructure and capital flow paths: After several years of development, BNB Chain's DeFi ecosystem is becoming increasingly complete, offering stablecoins, DEXs, lending, derivatives, and more. Therefore, tokens issued by traditional institutions can seamlessly connect to various on-chain application scenarios upon listing. For example, CMBMINT can be used for collateralized lending in DeFi protocols such as @VenusProtocol and @lista_dao. It can be said that BNB Chain possesses both technological advantages and a solid ecosystem foundation, meeting TradFi's performance and compliance requirements while providing broad user and scenario support, naturally becoming the preferred platform for traditional institutions entering the blockchain in 2025. III. BNB Chain's symbiotic ecosystem: How retail investors, institutions, and project teams can all benefit. With the entry of TradFi institutions, BNB Chain's user structure has become more complex. In 2025, an interesting phenomenon emerged: retail investors, institutions, and project teams operated independently on the same blockchain. Retail Investors: Still Active, Profit-Seeking Instinct Remains Undiminished. Even with a large influx of institutional investors, the enthusiasm of retail investors on the BNB Chain remained undiminished. In 2025, multiple rounds of meme coin price surges occurred on platforms like Four.meme, driving up BNB's on-chain trading volume and briefly propelling its price to a record high of nearly $1300. Furthermore, the PerpDex platform Aster offered high-leverage tools, and its low-gas, high-TPS environment allowed these users to easily execute multiple trades within a day. Beyond trading frequency, retail investors also gained passive income through strategies such as participating in on-chain vaults; for example, ListaDAO has launched over ten vaults including BNB, USD1, and USDT.

      Image: ListaDAO Vault Interface

      The existence of institutional products has not affected the trading experience of retail investors. On the contrary, due to the richer asset base and deeper capital pool, it has brought more possibilities for combination strategies.

      Institutions: Issuing Assets, Anchoring Returns

      After TradFi institutions landed on BNB Chain, they mainly played the role of issuers and capital providers. They brought real-world assets (such as fund shares, bonds, and stablecoins) onto the chain.

      On BNB Chain, institutional funds can flow on-chain in ways beyond simply subscribing to fund products. For example, the Venus protocol supports on-chain lending using RWA and stablecoins as collateral, reducing idle funds. In this process, on-chain automatic clearing, real-time settlement, and disintermediation significantly reduce the transaction and time costs of traditional finance. Project Teams: Hubs Connecting Retail Investor Traffic and Institutional Assets On one hand, native projects such as PancakeSwap, Lista DAO, and Venus continue to serve a large number of retail and crypto investors, providing them with decentralized trading, lending, and yield aggregation functions. On the other hand, these projects are actively connecting with institutionally issued RWA assets and interest-bearing stablecoins, creating opportunities for traditional assets to be used on-chain. For example, the aforementioned lending protocol Venus quickly supported CMBMINT fund tokens as collateral, and the yield protocol Lista DAO deeply integrated the institutional stablecoin USD1. What's even more remarkable is that this integration doesn't conflict with existing retail investors; instead, it expands the overall ecosystem. Overall, BNB Chain is one of the few ecosystems that allows retail investors, institutions, and project teams to each get what they need on the same chain, without interfering with each other, and to develop collaboratively: Retail investors bring trading activity and usage frequency; Institutions bring capital volume and asset diversity; Project teams design revenue structures, connecting the two into a system. The core reason all of this can coexist is that BNB Chain upholds maximum openness and compatibility. It's not a chain for a specific type of user, but rather an open platform that can accommodate different roles. IV. BNB Chain Project Transformation: From Traffic-Driven Competition to Real Revenue After the Meme craze subsided, in response to market changes, projects within the BNB Chain ecosystem have introduced more real-revenue models: ListaDAO: Paired with stablecoins such as USD1 and U, it has created a collateral-lending-interest-earning capital cycle system, with a TVL exceeding $2 billion. Aster: Starting with perpetual contracts, it expanded to on-chain US stock derivatives trading, and then issued the USDF series of stablecoins. Aster does not solely rely on transaction fee revenue, but rather transforms into a platform with a diversified financial product line. PancakeSwap: As the oldest DEX on the BNB Chain, PancakeSwap is also quietly transforming. It's reducing CAKE inflation, retiring outdated staking products, launching perpetual stock contracts, and announcing the launch of the AI-driven prediction market Probable. Behind these changes, three major signals of ecosystem restructuring on the BNB Chain are gradually emerging: 1. Decreasing Meme Traffic in the Crypto Market; Users Are More Concerned About the Feasibility of Yield Structures. The past methods of relying on issuing tokens to generate hype and accumulating trading volume are becoming increasingly difficult to retain funds. Users are starting to focus on whether the returns are real, rather than how appealing the narrative is. 2. Integrating with Real-World Assets to Build a Robust and Sustainable Product Structure. As we can see, on-chain products are proactively integrating with traditional funds to meet users' dual needs for stable returns and institutions' needs for compliant assets. 3. Protocol Collaboration Begins, Moving Towards Organic Symbiosis. A stablecoin can simultaneously participate in lending, mining, and staking, meaning asset paths are no longer isolated. This also signifies that projects on BNB Chain are collaboratively building a robust on-chain network. V. Outlook for 2026: BNB Chain's Next Stage Role. With 2026 approaching, the environment facing BNB Chain has quietly changed. The decline in Meme popularity is merely a symptom; a deeper challenge lies in the reshaping of the industry landscape: New user growth may be entering a plateau. The past reliance on Memes to drive traffic is unlikely to be replicable, and the revenue strategies of newly entering institutions have not yet been fully validated, potentially leading to a slowdown in new user growth. Against this backdrop, BNB Chain's path for 2026 is becoming increasingly clear: 1. The Preferred Channel for On-Chain Real-World Assets BNB Chain has largely validated its ability to support high-frequency trading and stable assets, providing traditional institutions with a "secure, controllable, and low-friction" channel for on-chain asset deployment. With the growth of stablecoin market capitalization, the intensive implementation of RWA projects, and more countries and financial institutions adopting an open attitude towards on-chain assets, BNB Chain is expected to become one of the priority infrastructure platforms considered in the Asia-Pacific region and even globally. 2. Testing Ground for New Tracks: Prediction Markets and Privacy Modules Besides stablecoins and RWA, BNB Chain is also exploring more future-oriented tracks. On BNB Chain, prediction markets are experiencing a diversified boom: @opinionlabsxyz, with its macro trading perspective, has risen to become a leading platform; @predictdotfun innovatively uses prediction positions for DeFi operations, improving capital efficiency; @0xProbable provides a zero-fee experience, focusing on lightweight and high-frequency events; @42 forges real-world events into tradable assets, pioneering a new "event asset" model; and @Bentodotfun is attempting to gamify prediction markets, creating a new interactive form that is composable and social. Furthermore, in 2025, privacy coins outperformed the mainstream market, and their on-chain usage share steadily increased. In 2026, BNB Chain is expected to introduce privacy technologies such as zero-knowledge proofs, helping financial institutions to manage the isolation of some private data on-chain. 3. Continuous Protocol Layer Optimization to Lower the Barrier to Entry Technically, BNB Chain will continue to upgrade its underlying protocol in 2026. For example: The goal is to process 20,000 transactions per second, with confirmation time reduced to almost instantaneous, potentially leading to cheaper gas fees in the future. Privacy Framework: A configurable, compliant privacy mechanism that supports high-frequency trading and ordinary transfers while maintaining DeFi composability. AI Agent Framework: Provides an AI Agent identity registration system, reputation scoring, etc. When retail investor funds meet long-term institutional funds, when on-chain native returns meet off-chain real cash flow, what sparks will fly? BNB Chain's performance in 2025 provides a positive answer. Moving forward to 2026, turning a new page. Coexistence may become the next key word in crypto, and BNB Chain is undoubtedly one of the most watched testing grounds.

data.title: My Personal Account: Three Years of Losing Tens of Millions in Crypto VC – A Heartbreaking Lesson
data.description:
Author: BruceLLBlue

Recently, Twitter has been buzzing with posts from Chinese-speaking KOLs showing off how much they "earned in the past year": millions, tens of millions, 102.4 billion (don't run away if you're just making jokes)... After seeing all this, all I can say is, amazing! But as a former VC investment manager (GP, General Partner), I just want to complain: After working in Crypto VC for several years, I lost tens of millions of dollars. This isn't just empty talk; it's a real history of blood and tears. 3 years, 55+ investments, 27 losses (including rugs), 15 total losses, and I even invested in 9 relatively top-tier VCs.

Among them, NFT-related projects were completely wiped out, with GameFi suffering a 33% loss, and Infra being the hardest hit, with many projects reduced to only 10%-20% of their original valuation. Congratulations to the KOLs and esteemed crypto traders who are showing off their income; you caught the secondary market boom. But what about the ordinary VCs focused on primary market investments? They're constantly praising project teams; waiting 3-4 years for unlocking their investments, often ending up with "invested too early, invested correctly, but unable to exit." Why the opposite of what we call losses? Because this isn't about complaining about being poor, but a wake-up call. Crypto VC is inherently difficult; bear markets are brutal, and bull markets are often "harvested" by project teams. But I believe that in this new cycle, continuing as a VC (or evolving into one) may not be the best time. Although large institutional funds are entering the market, regulations are becoming clearer, and AI + on-chain tools are reshaping exit paths, I believe there are better ways and paths to realize self-worth. I'm sharing my hard-earned lessons for everyone's benefit. Lesson 1: The Naked Statistics: The Truth About the "Win Rate" of 55 Deals From joining Crypto VC in August 2022 to leaving in July 2025, I personally handled 55 direct investments and invested in 9 funds. Rugs accounted for 14/55 (25.45%): The hardest hit areas were NFT projects, all of which went to zero. One "star project" backed by a big IP experienced early NFT hype, but the team had little Web3 experience, and the founder, a top celebrity, showed little interest in issuing tokens. After the core members left, the project became a soft rug. Another "music + Web3" project, after leaving a giant company, worked on the project for several years, but achieved nothing and quietly died out. There are also Dex projects with "executives' entrepreneurial dreams": founders make their teams work illegally while they collect the money, and core employees run away; "potential stocks" from a university lab have basically all failed.

Losses account for 28/55 (50.1%): One GameFi project, after launching at 5x, ended up in a mess (only 20% of the cost remained, a 99% drop); another GameFi project, "produced by a team with a North American tech company background," peaked at 12x, but is now only 10% of its cost; yet another GameFi project was tricked by a CEX's Launchpad, causing a lot of token dumping pressure, and failed to gain traction.

The infrastructure sector fared even worse: no breakthroughs in the ecosystem, no technological innovation, and after the hype died down, many projects were left with only 10% of their original cost; without timely hedging, they were practically wiped out. Another example is the SocialFi project within the MOVE ecosystem, which collapsed just before the 2024 bull market. So what about fund of funds (FoF) investments? We invested in 9 top-tier European and American funds, including @hack_vc, @Maven11Capital, @FigmentCapital, @IOSGVC, and @BanklessVC. These funds have participated in the early-stage investments of many well-known projects during this cycle, such as @eigenlayer, @babylonlabs_io, @MorphoLabs, @movementlabsxyz, @ionet, @alt_layer, @MYX_Finance, @solayer_labs, @ethsign, @0G_labs, @berachain, @initia, @stable, @monad, @ether_fi, @brevis_zk, and @SentientAGI. On paper, the return looks decent at 2-3x, seemingly respectable, but the actual realized return (DPI) is estimated to be only 1-1.5x. Why this expectation? The main reason is the slow unlocking of projects and poor market liquidity. If a bear market or a crash similar to FTX occurs, the positions could be wiped out instantly. Lesson Two: The Deeper the Pit, the Deeper the Human Nature — Several Tragic Cases That Moved Me The most heartbreaking is the disastrous outcome of "investing in people": One Dex project, whose founder appeared to have the aura of a CEX executive, actually outsourced illegal labor to the team, pocketing the profits; GameFi's "North American giant dream" saw its price steadily decline after launching at 12x, never recovering. An Infra project founded by someone from @0xPolygon has no hope of ecosystem breakthrough, only retaining 15% of its initial investment valuation; several popular Infra projects launched on the two Korean giants (Upbit and Bithumb), then plummeted, never recovering. There was even a "music NFT" project founded by a Tencent Music executive who, after several years of playing the soft rug, achieved nothing. VCs in the Chinese-speaking region suffer even more: Language, mindset, and resources are inherent disadvantages. Western funds operate on a completely different model; they focus on scale to earn management fees, while we operate with short-sighted quick flips and paper-hands. Well-known projects, after raising huge sums, outsource the implementation of roadmaps globally (I've contacted several; as long as the funding is sufficient), and the founders only need to build the community and raise more money. VCs, on the other hand, are the most vulnerable group. Some projects airdrop their assets, trading via USB drives and Korean exchanges (they pump and dump the profits after reaching the target price, which is why Korean exchanges often have opening premiums). Investors have no way to verify this. Every VC thinks they're amazing; checking these funds' IRR and DPI is less worthwhile than investing in USDT/USDC fixed deposits. Lesson Three: After Losing So Much, I Learned the Evolution of "Exit is King" Being a VC is truly difficult. You have to survive bear markets, gamble on people's hearts, understand human nature, and wait for your coins to unlock. It's a 3-4 year cycle, and without hedging/liquidity management in the secondary market, excess returns are virtually impossible. In my experience, most projects that earned excess returns were invested in at the end of 2022-2023 after the FTX crash. The core reasons were: low project valuations, strong founder conviction, and the right investment timing (the project had enough time to explore and achieve TGE early when the bull market arrived). Why did other projects perform poorly or even lose money? The main reasons were either too expensive, too early, or misaligned unlocking. Reflecting on this carefully, these are all valuable lessons! Moreover, with $BTC constantly hitting new highs, traditional giants and Wall Street are rushing in, and the window for ordinary people to get rich quick is actually narrowing. Institutional investment returns are converging with those of Web2 venture capital (it's unlikely we'll return to the era of rampant growth before 2021). The new generation of investors: They aren't necessarily VCs; they're more likely to be individual angel investors or super KOLs. Through their influence and resources, they can often obtain better unlocking solutions and higher-priced tokens than VCs. And it's not just about investing early and correctly; it's about capturing the entire chain: primary + secondary + options/convertible bonds + airdrop interaction + market making hedging + DeFi arbitrage. There are actually serious cognitive misalignments and differences between the East and West, which is a goldmine for arbitrage. Turning around and picking up the keyboard, I'm embarking on a dignified path of alpha creation through content output. My years in Crypto VC, where I lost tens of millions, taught me one thing: constantly fawning over project teams, waiting for token unlocks, and gambling on people's hearts and minds only earned me the lowly label of "VC dog" and the complaints of my backers. Project teams could secretly airdrop tokens while investors could only watch helplessly. Enough! Now, I've chosen to turn to writing: by typing away, I'll share industry insights and alpha predictions, no longer agonizing over waiting for project unlocks, but proactively positioning myself and seizing project opportunities. Compared to the passive waiting of VCs, this path is more dignified; I can freely output value, and the compounding effect comes from readers' trust and sharing. Ultimately, these past few years have taught me: Patience > Opportunity, Luck > Professionalism, FOMO = Suicide.

data.title: 2025 Annual Summary and 2026 Industry Outlook
data.description:
Authors: Binance Research (Moulik Nagesh, Joshua Wong, Michael JJ, Asher Lin Jiayong)

2025 was a year of both milestone achievements and divergent market performance for the crypto industry. The total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies surpassed $4 trillion for the first time, with Bitcoin (BTC) reaching a new all-time high. This was driven by continued institutional investment, progress in regulatory policies—particularly in the stablecoin sector—and the expanding availability of compliant investment products. Simultaneously, macroeconomic uncertainties stemming from monetary policy adjustments, trade frictions, and geopolitical risks dominated market trends throughout the year, causing significant price volatility in crypto assets and recurring risk aversion. This resulted in a trading range of approximately 76% for cryptocurrencies throughout the year, with the total market capitalization fluctuating wildly between $2.4 trillion and $4.2 trillion. Despite structural breakthroughs in market access and infrastructure development, the cryptocurrency market ultimately declined by approximately 7.9% for the year.

This result highlights that in 2025, the pricing logic of crypto assets is increasingly influenced by the macroeconomic environment and traditional financial cycles, rather than simply determined by the industry's own application implementation progress. From a macro perspective, the key words for 2025 are "data fog" and volatility. The market experienced a series of events during that year, including the change of government in the United States, the impact of "Tariff Liberation Day," and the government shutdown, all of which severely disrupted the accuracy of economic signals. Although the artificial intelligence (AI) concept boom and the Debt Ceiling and Budget Act (OBBBA) propelled Bitcoin prices to new highs in the early second half of the year, by the end of 2025, due to delays in the implementation of regulatory policies, cryptocurrency trends decoupled from the rebound of traditional assets. However, the market outlook for 2026 foreshadows a clear "reboot of risky assets," driven by the "three pillars of policy": globally synchronized monetary easing policies, large-scale fiscal stimulus mainly through cash handouts and tax rebates, and a new wave of regulatory deregulation. This shift is expected to drive the market's funding structure from being dominated by retail speculators to being dominated by institutional funds. Meanwhile, the potential implementation of the US Bitcoin strategic reserve will inject liquidity into the cryptocurrency market, helping the industry usher in a new round of expansion. The market performance of Bitcoin exhibits a significant structural divergence: the level of capital activity at the market level is at odds with the economic activity on the underlying blockchain. Although Bitcoin hit a new all-time high this year, its closing price fell slightly at the end of the year, underperforming gold and most major stock indices. However, its market capitalization remained stable at around $1.8 trillion, with its market dominance maintained between 58% and 60%. Even with weak price performance, the trend of funds concentrating on Bitcoin is intensifying: US spot ETFs saw net inflows of over $21 billion, and corporate holdings exceeded 1.1 million BTC, accounting for 5.5% of the total Bitcoin supply. The network security of Bitcoin continues to strengthen, with peak hashrate exceeding 1 ZH/second and mining difficulty increasing by approximately 36% year-on-year, data confirming miners' long-term investment confidence. In contrast, Bitcoin's on-chain activity has weakened: the number of active addresses decreased by approximately 16% year-over-year, the number of transactions failed to surpass the peak of the previous bull market, and speculative token trading only exhibited brief, non-sustainable pulse-like price movements. These signals collectively indicate that Bitcoin's liquidity supply, pricing mechanism, and market demand are increasingly being realized through off-chain financial channels and long-term holding behavior, with the underlying public chain playing only a secondary role. This trend further solidifies Bitcoin's positioning as a macro-financial asset, rather than a network centered on transaction functionality. In the public chain (L1) sector, market practice in 2025 proved that simple on-chain activity is not a reliable indicator of its economic value—many public chains failed to convert user traffic into transaction fee revenue, value capture capabilities, and sustained positive token performance. Meanwhile, the market structure of the public chain sector continues to concentrate on a few leading projects. Ethereum maintains its dominant position due to its active developer ecosystem, decentralized finance (DeFi) liquidity, and total asset value. However, its relative returns lag behind Bitcoin due to the shrinking transaction capacity of its underlying chain and the fee compression effect caused by Layer 2 networks. Solana, on the other hand, maintained high transaction throughput and daily active user scale throughout the year, achieving substantial growth in stablecoin issuance. Even after the speculative frenzy subsided, it still generated considerable protocol revenue and successfully obtained approval for a US spot ETF, further enhancing institutional accessibility. BNB Chain, leveraging market narratives and a large retail trading base, drove significant growth in on-chain spot and derivatives trading, stablecoin settlement volume, and Real-World Asset (RWA) deployments. Its native token, BNB, became the best-performing mainstream crypto asset of the year. The core lesson from the public blockchain landscape in 2025 is that the competitive advantage of public blockchains increasingly depends on their ability to monetize continuous cash flows—such as transactions, payments, or institutional settlements—rather than simply maximizing the number of transactions. In 2025, the Ethereum Layer 2 (L2) network ecosystem handled over 90% of Ethereum-based transactions. This achievement benefited from the increased block space resulting from protocol upgrades and the reduction in data availability (DA) costs. As transaction execution functions migrate off-chain, the core issue of industry focus shifted to whether the scale effect of Layer 2 networks could translate into sustained user stickiness, transaction fee revenue, and economic synergy with the underlying public chains. In reality, the Layer 2 network ecosystem exhibits significant differentiation: transaction volume, liquidity, and transaction fee revenue are highly concentrated in a few optimistic rolling networks, represented by Base and Arbitrum; meanwhile, some dedicated application chains with clear application scenarios and superior user experiences also performed exceptionally well. Most other Layer 2 network projects experienced a precipitous drop in user activity after the incentive policies were withdrawn. Zero-knowledge (ZK) rolling networks have made continuous progress in proof efficiency and decentralization, but still lag behind optimistic rolling networks by an order of magnitude in terms of total value locked (TVL) and transaction fee revenue. Currently, the core constraints facing the Layer 2 network sector include: market fragmentation caused by over a hundred rolling networks, diminishing marginal utility of incentive mechanisms, and uneven decentralization of sorters. In 2025, the DeFi industry took another crucial step towards "structural institutionalization," shifting its focus to improving capital efficiency and compliance. The total value locked in the industry stabilized at $124.4 billion, with a significant shift in its capital composition. The proportion of stablecoins and interest-bearing assets increased substantially, while the proportion of inflationary tokens continued to decline. That year, the total value locked in real-world assets (RWA) reached $17 billion, historically surpassing decentralized exchanges (DEXs), driven by the widespread adoption of tokenized government bonds and stocks. Simultaneously, the U.S. Genius Act provided a clear regulatory framework for stablecoins, pushing the total market capitalization of stablecoins past $307 billion, officially making them a core global settlement infrastructure. Functionally, decentralized finance (DeFi) has evolved into a cash flow creation giant, with annual protocol revenue soaring to $16.2 billion, comparable to mainstream traditional financial institutions. Governance tokens have thus transformed into "blue-chip assets" with stable returns. The dominance of on-chain transactions has been further strengthened, with the peak share of spot trading volume on decentralized and centralized exchanges approaching 20%. 2025 can be considered a "breakthrough year for mainstreaming" for stablecoins. Driven by the landmark regulatory clarity brought by the GENIUS Act and the entry of institutional funds, the total market capitalization of stablecoins surged by nearly 50%, exceeding $305 billion. Daily trading volume of stablecoins soared by 26% to $3.54 trillion, a figure far exceeding Visa's $1.34 trillion, fully demonstrating the superiority of stablecoins in the field of high-speed cross-border payments. The industry's growth is driven by a number of emerging leading projects: six new stablecoins—BUIDL, PYUSD, RLUSD, USD1, USDf, and USDtB—each with a market capitalization exceeding $1 billion, have brought a new competitive landscape and real-world application scenarios to the market. These developments have laid a solid foundation for the continued expansion of stablecoins in areas such as payments, savings, and fintech. Consumer-grade crypto applications are entering a critical development phase: blockchain infrastructure is maturing, and the industry's focus is shifting from technological research and development to real-world application implementation and user experience optimization. Leading this transformation are various digital banks and fintech platforms—both Web2 giants and native Web3 companies—that are rapidly evolving into full-featured banking service providers based on blockchain technology. Although the market enthusiasm for crypto games and social applications has cooled somewhat this year, the deep integration of blockchain technology with the global payments and fintech industries has laid a crucial foundation for the emergence of a new generation of native networks in these sectors. These new networks will be designed around transparency and verifiability in their underlying architecture. As the industry's focus shifts from infrastructure construction to application-driven growth, its core mission is also evolving: from simply pursuing decentralization to meticulously building systems that combine credibility and verifiability, thereby gaining the trust of both consumers and institutional users. In 2025, cutting-edge technology development focused on the integrated innovation of three major technologies: AI agents, on-chain payments, and decentralized collaboration of real-world infrastructure. The most groundbreaking advancement was the realization of internet-scale application of an agent payment system based on the HTTP native settlement standard (which reactivated the functionality of the HTTP 402 "Payment Request" response code), providing a pay-per-use monetization model for application programming interfaces (APIs), data services, and automated workflows. By the end of the year, the payment network had processed over 100 million transactions, with a cumulative transaction volume exceeding $30 million, and daily active transactions consistently exceeding 1 million, with over 90% of transactions initiated by agents. Meanwhile, Decentralized Entity Artificial Intelligence (DePAI), an extension of the Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network (DePIN), is emerging, aiming to achieve collaborative scheduling of autonomous machines. However, the bottleneck for development in this field in 2025 is not the design of token economic models, but rather limitations imposed by data quality, the gap between simulation environments and real-world scenarios, high capital investment thresholds, and security and regulatory compliance requirements. In contrast, DeFAI and DeSci are still in the exploratory stage and have not yet demonstrated sustainable economic output capabilities compared to native payments by intelligent agents and early machine economic applications. The core characteristic of institutional cryptocurrency applications in 2025 is that crypto assets are no longer simply used as price exposure tools, but are deeply integrated into the core financial business processes of institutions. Commercial banks are accelerating the development of crypto asset-backed lending businesses, signifying that Bitcoin (and selected Ethereum) are being increasingly recognized by institutions as financial-grade collateral under custody and compliance frameworks. At the same time, the variety and structure of compliant cryptocurrency ETFs continue to expand, further solidifying ETFs as the preferred channel for institutional investment in crypto assets. Tokenized money market funds have become a mature application scenario in the field of real-world asset tokenization. Leveraging their advantages of fast settlement, high collateral liquidity, and strong auditability, they have rapidly risen to become on-chain cash equivalents. Meanwhile, the scale of corporate digital asset vaults (DATs) has expanded significantly, but the market performance in 2025 also highlighted the sustainability pressures facing this sector: the returns of leveraged vault products were lower than those of interest-bearing ETFs with simpler structures. This phenomenon indicates that institutional crypto asset applications are shifting from simple asset accumulation to infrastructure and return-oriented deployments. Global cryptocurrency regulatory frameworks are gradually moving towards complementarity and synergy amidst differentiated development: the US promoted industry innovation through the GENIUS Act in July, establishing the first federal-level stablecoin regulatory framework; the EU formally implemented the Crypto Asset Market Regulation (MiCA), enacting a strict licensing system; Hong Kong, with its Stablecoin Ordinance and supporting tax incentives, consolidated its position as a crypto asset hub; and Singapore introduced stricter compliance and licensing rules in June, further raising industry entry standards. At the international level, the implementation of the OECD's Crypto Asset Reporting Framework (CARF) has accelerated across countries, laying a standardized foundation for global crypto asset tax transparency and cross-border information exchange. Looking ahead to 2026, the development prospects of several key sectors are highly promising, and we expect significant breakthroughs in these areas within the year. These themes cover multiple narrative directions and sub-sectors, including the macro environment and Bitcoin trends, institutional application implementation, policy and regulatory evolution, stablecoin innovation, asset tokenization, decentralized trading, and prediction markets.

data.title: Wall Street is entering the prediction market with a $200,000 annual salary offer.
data.description:
Written by: Niu Keke, Deep Tide TechFlow
It's finally here. The prediction market, once built by political supporters, speculative retail investors, and "wool party" (a term referring to those who exploit loopholes for profit), is welcoming a group of silent but deadly new players.

According to a report in the Financial Times on Thursday, several well-known trading firms, including DRW, Susquehanna, and Tyr Capital, are building dedicated prediction market trading teams.

DRW posted job advertisements last week, offering traders with a base annual salary of up to $200,000 for "real-time monitoring and trading of active markets" on platforms such as Polymarket and Kalshi.

Options trading giant Susquehanna is recruiting prediction market traders, requiring them to be able to "detect incorrect fair value," identify "abnormal behavior" and "inefficiencies" in the prediction market, and is also building a dedicated sports trading team.

Crypto hedge fund Tyr Capital continues to recruit prediction market traders who are already running complex strategies. Data supports this expansionary ambition. Monthly trading volume surged from less than $100 million at the beginning of 2024 to over $8 billion in December 2025, with a record-breaking $701.7 million traded on January 12th. When the pool of funds becomes deep enough to support the size of giants, the entry of Wall Street becomes inevitable. Arbitrage First. In prediction markets, institutions and retail investors are playing completely different games. Retail investors often rely on fragmented information to predict individual events, which is essentially gambling, while institutional players focus on cross-platform arbitrage and structural market opportunities. In October 2025, Boaz Weinstein, founder of hedge fund Saba Capital Management, stated in a closed-door meeting that predictive markets allow portfolio managers to hedge investments with greater accuracy, especially regarding the probability of specific events. Standing next to Polymarket CEO Shayne Coplan, he said, “A few months ago, Polymarket showed a 50% probability of a recession, while the credit market showed a risk of about 2%. You can think of countless pairing trades that couldn’t be done before.” According to Weinstein, hedge fund managers can buy “no recession” contracts on Polymarket because the market perceives a 50% probability of recession, making the contract relatively cheap. Meanwhile, in the credit market, one can short some bonds or credit products that will plummet during an economic recession, because the credit market only accounts for a 2% probability of a recession, so these products are still highly priced. If an economic recession does occur, you might lose a small amount of money in the polymarket, but make a large profit in the credit market because those overvalued bonds will crash. If there is no recession, you might make money in the polymarket and suffer a small loss in the credit market, but overall you will still profit. The emergence of prediction markets has given traditional financial markets a completely new "price discovery tool." The arrival of a privileged class further tilts the balance. Susquehanna is Kalshi's first market maker and has reached a tournament contract agreement with Robinhood. Kalshi offers market makers numerous benefits: lower fees, special trading limits, and more convenient trading channels; specific terms have not been disclosed. The entry of market makers will quickly change the market. Previously, the prediction market often suffered from insufficient liquidity, especially for niche events. When you wanted to buy or sell large contracts, you might face large spreads or simply not find a counterparty. Professional institutions will quickly eliminate obvious pricing errors. For example, price differences for the same event on different platforms, or obviously unreasonable probability pricing, will be quickly smoothed out. This is not good news for retail investors. Previously, you might have found that "Trump winning" had a 60% probability on Polymarket and a 55% probability on Kalshi, allowing for simple arbitrage. In the future, such opportunities will essentially disappear. With Wall Street PhDs earning hundreds of thousands of dollars annually, future predictive contracts may also enter an era of specialization and diversification, moving beyond just single-event predictions. Examples include: 1. Multi-event combination contracts, similar to parlay betting in sports betting; 2. Time series contracts, predicting the probability of an event occurring within a specific time period; 3. Conditional probability products, determining the probability of B occurring if A occurs… Looking back at financial history, from forex to futures to cryptocurrencies, the development of every emerging market follows a similar trajectory: retail investors ignite the spark, eventually leading to institutional dominance. The prediction market is repeating this process. Technological advantage, capital size, and privileged access will ultimately determine who survives this game of probability. For retail investors, while there may still be a glimmer of hope in long-term predictions or niche areas, they must recognize reality: as Wall Street's sophisticated machines operate at full speed, the era of easily profiting from information asymmetry may be over.

data.title: Germany’s Second-Largest Bank DZ Bank Receives MiCA Approval to Launch MeinKrypto for Retail Crypto Trading
data.description:
DZ Bank Opens Retail Crypto Trading Through Cooperative Banks With MiCA Approval

DZ Bank, Germany’s second-largest lender by assets, has secured regulatory approval from the Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin) under the European Union’s Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework, setting the stage for a retail-focused crypto trading platform across the cooperative banking sector.

JUST IN: 🇩🇪 Germany’s second-largest bank DZ Bank gets approval for institutional Bitcoin and crypto trading.

Bullish 🚀 pic.twitter.com/MemVrk9W3k

— Bitcoin Magazine (@BitcoinMagazine) January 14, 2026

How Will MeinKrypto Change Retail Banking Access to Crypto

The newly approved platform, meinKrypto, is designed for customers of Volksbanken and Raiffeisenbanken, allowing them to buy, sell, and hold cryptocurrencies entirely within the regulated banking environment.

Integrated directly into the VR Banking App, the service provides wallets and access to trading bitcoin (BTC), ether (ETH), litecoin (LTC) and cardano (ADA).

DZ Bank acts as the central institution supporting the network, but each cooperative bank must submit its own MiCAR notification to BaFin before activating the platform for its customers.

Germany’s DZ Bank just opened crypto trading to millions of mainstream banking customers. Its new meinKrypto platform lets users buy and hold $BTC, $ETH, $LTC and $ADA directly in the VR Banking App — a major push to make crypto feel like regular finance 🚀…

— invezz.com (@InvezzPortal) January 14, 2026

Once approved and implemented, retail clients will be able to invest in digital assets without relying on external exchanges.

Who Will Provide Custody and Trading Services

The technical infrastructure for meinKrypto was developed by Atruvia, the IT provider for the cooperative financial group.

Custody of digital assets will be handled by Boerse Stuttgart Digital, while trade execution will be managed by EUWAX AG.

Each cooperative bank will determine individually whether to offer the service, allowing for a staggered rollout based on strategy and risk considerations.

Why Cooperative Banks Are Embracing Crypto Now

The move reflects rising interest among Germany’s cooperative banks to enter the crypto market.

A survey conducted by Genoverband in September 2025 found that more than 71% of cooperative banks were considering offering crypto services to retail clients, up from 54% the previous year.

Around one-third of those banks indicated plans to launch services within five months, highlighting tangible market demand.

Krypto zieht in die Bankenwelt ein.

Eine aktuelle Genoverband-Studie zeigt: 71 % der Volks- und Raiffeisenbanken befassen sich mit Krypto-Handel – ein klarer Sprung im Vergleich zu 54 % im Vorjahr.

🚀 Erste Banken wollen schon in wenigen Monaten starten.
⏳ Andere rechnen mit… pic.twitter.com/IclfviKe5Y

— KM Intelligent Investments (@KM_Investments) September 21, 2025

From Institutional Testing to Retail Adoption

DZ Bank has previously focused on institutional clients, offering digital asset services through partnerships with regulated market infrastructure providers such as Boerse Stuttgart Digital.

The MiCAR license now extends this approach to retail customers, signalling a shift from experimental offerings to integration into mainstream financial services.

Will Digital Assets Become Part of Everyday Banking

The rollout follows a decentralised model where each cooperative bank decides individually whether to provide access to meinKrypto.

Customers can manage digital assets entirely digitally, keeping all transactions within the regulated banking system.
